claim: UNESCO BR cross-currency drift bug = gate-input asymmetry across the 15-member ZFIX_EXCHANGERATE composite. Camp A (CHECK_CONS / EF_FUND_RESERVATION / FUNDBLOCK) gates on FR's own EUR currency and always fires. Camp B (PAYCOMMIT / NEW_ITEM / AVC / KBLEW / FI) gates on per-row posting currency and silently skips when posting is non-EUR. The two camps thus produce divergent ledger states: FR side at BR (1.09529), consumption side at identity / M-rate. AVC pool drifts fund-by-fund until cover-group hits zero headroom => FMAVC005.

claim: AVC available is a fund/cover-group level metric, not an FR-line-level metric. All FR lines on the same (FONDS, FUNDSCTR, CMMTITEM-rolled-to-control-object) draw from a single shared AVC pool. The pool is calculated as: Available = FMAVCT.allotment - sum(open FMIOI commitments) - sum(FMIFIIT actuals) on the same key. FMAVC005 fires when Available <= 0 + new posting amount > 0. The triggering FR line is incidental; the constraint is the fund pool.

claim: YRGGBS00 UXR1 (XREF1 substitution, step 005) has DOUBLE ASYMMETRY vs UXR2 (XREF2, step 006): (1) CODE-LEVEL: line 998 has `IF bseg_xref1 = space` guard commented out, while UXR2 line 1026 keeps its guard intact; (2) CONFIG-LEVEL: UXR1 has NO declarative prerequisite in GB901 — fires unconditionally for every BSEG line, while UXR2 has prereq `HKONT IN ('1375012','1375022','1375032','1375052','1341011','1341021')` restricting to 6 specific GL accounts. Combined effect: UXR1 fires on EVERY posting line of EVERY user at UNESCO and unconditionally overwrites user-typed XREF1 with USR05.Y_USERFO. This is the root cause of the systemic XREF drift pattern observed in DQ-019 (21,754 manual Q1 2026 XREF edits via FBL3N/FB02/FBL5N). The manual edits are predominantly on XREF1 (universal), not XREF2 (targeted 6 accounts).

claim: YTBAE002 has an empty-RANGE-to-unbounded-LDB-scan defect at `YTBAE002.abap:1366-1370`. The LOOP AT LR_SAKNR that APPENDs each local SAKNR range entry to the COSEL/RSPARAMS table has NO `IS NOT INITIAL` guard. When the upstream SKB1 SELECT at `YTBAE002.abap:1098-1101` (WHERE BUKRS+HBKID+HKTID) returns zero rows that both (a) have SAKNR+3(2) IN ('11','13','15') AND (b) have XOPVW='X' (see IF chain at lines 1112-1118), the GR_SAKNR_OI range is empty, the loop iterates zero times, zero `SD_SAKNR` entries are pushed to LT_PARAMS, and `CALL FUNCTION 'LDB_PROCESS' LDBNAME='SDF'` at `YTBAE002.abap:1509-1517` runs BSIS with only `SD_BUKRS + SD_GJAHR + SD_STIDA + SD_CURTP` as filters. In SAP behavior an empty IN-list means no filter, so the LDB scans BSIS unrestricted by HKONT. At UNESCO that is 228,242 BSIS rows for BUKRS='UNES' GJAHR='2026' (live P01 RFC probe 2026-04-20), driving SAPDBSDF:1983 PUT_BSIS past the dialog `rdisp/max_wprun_time` cap and terminating with runtime error TIME_OUT. Same program runs fine in batch (5 successful TBTCO runs March 2026, Status=F) because batch has no work-process time cap. This is the PRIMARY root-cause mechanism behind J_DAVANE's 2026-04-20 13:51:49 dump under TCODE YTR3.

Class-generalization scan of the full Y-stack found Claim 53 (empty-RANGE → unbounded-LDB-scan) to be UNIQUE to YTBAE002. YTBAE001 + YTBAE001_HR use a completely different selection mechanism: PERFORM 01_SELECT_BSIS in include YTBAM003 iterates TSAKO config-table (YTBAM003.abap:53-59) and is safe-by-construction (empty TSAKO → zero SELECTs, no unbounded scan). YFI_BANK_RECONCILIATION (2023) delegates to YCL_FI_BANK_RECONCILIATION_BL whose selection is user-controlled via S_HKONT SELECT-OPTION (YFI_BANK_RECONCILIATION_SEL.abap:6) — different design entirely. Therefore Claim 53 does NOT auto-extend to the rest of the family.

resolution_notes: Session #058: DEMOTED from primary root cause to latent defect. Additionally, live BSIS probe on 0001194424 returned only 13 rows, which cannot TIME_OUT on volume alone. Decisive evidence: user reproduced the program in ~2 seconds from HQ (fast LAN) with no dump. The network axis isolates the real mechanism (MODE 'E' BDC — see Claim 54). The defect at :1366-1370 is REAL but LATENT (never triggered J_DAVANE's dump). Kept as preventive-audit class.

claim: CALL TRANSACTION ... USING <bdc> MODE 'E' inside a GET/LOOP over large bank-reconciliation data is NETWORK-COUPLED: any BDC error opens SAPGUI on the user's workstation and the cumulative GUI round-trip on slow WAN paths (UNESCO field offices) exceeds rdisp/max_wprun_time, producing a TIME_OUT in the caller's fetch loop with a stack that blames the LDB rather than the CALL TRANSACTION. Observed at YTBAE002.abap:27 (CONSTANTS: GC_MOD TYPE C VALUE 'E') + four CALL TRANSACTION sites inside the GET bsis / LDB_PROCESS loop: :723 (FB08 reverse), :771 (F-04 post-with-clearing), :819 (FBRA reset cleared), :853 (FB08 reverse alt branch) — all passing MODE GC_MOD and already collecting messages into Y_MESSTAB / GT_RECONCIL_MESS. Canonical fix: change MODE from 'E' to 'N' (silent). Errors remain visible in the captured Y_MESSTAB → GT_RECONCIL_MESS log, no functional regression. One-char fix file written at Zagentexecution/fixes/INC-000006906/YTBAE002_fix.abap.

All four inside the GET bsis/LDB_PROCESS iteration body. Lines 754, 795, 840, 874: PROC_RECONCIL_MESS_ADD persists Y_MESSTAB to GT_RECONCIL_MESS (silent-capture path already present).

claim: BCM routing at UNESCO is triggered by tcode OBPM5 configuration (table TFIBLMPAYBLOCK, 2 rows): LAUFK=' '/P + LAUFI='*' + XBATCH='X' — wildcard routes EVERY F110 and Payroll run into BCM evaluation. Downstream filters reduce actual BCM batch creation: (1) PM must be DMEE-enabled (T042Z.XBKKT='X' — 100% clean cut: no XBKKT='' ever creates a BCM batch), (2) co code ICTP is structurally excluded (0/603 runs), (3) BCM grouping rules (IMG > FSCM > BCM > Payment Grouping > Rule Maintenance) evaluate and assign one of 14 UNESCO-custom RULE_IDs (UNES_AP_ST, UNES_TR_TR, PAYROLL, IIEP_AP_ST, UIS_AP_MAX, UBO_AP_MAX, UIL_AP_ST, UNES_AP_10/11/EX/IK, UNES_AR_BP, UBO_AP_ST, UIS_AP_ST). The observation 'LAUFI ending in B ↔ BCM batch' (100% correlation) is a SYMPTOM of UNESCO naming convention for BCM-destined F110 variants combined with the OBPM5 wildcard match, NOT a SAP routing rule. Supersedes legacy brain memory claim 'LAUFI prefix B=BCM, 0/T/M=Direct' (falsified: prefix-B runs have 0% BCM linkage; prefix-0 runs with suffix-B have 100%).

evidence_for.cite: BNK_BATCH_HEADER: 27,443 batches 2014-2026, 100% have LAUFI ending in B. REGUH (full schema extract 15mo, 505,636 rows, 9,163 distinct runs): XBKKT='' → 0/2,941 BCM (100% clean negative), XBKKT='X' → 1,045/6,222 BCM (16.8%). Co code ICTP: 0/603 BCM. Companies UNES/IIEP/UIL/UBO/UIS: 10-29% BCM within XBKKT='X'.

Full config (2 rows only): {MANDT=350, LAUFK=' ', LAUFI='*', XBATCH='X'} and {MANDT=350, LAUFK='P', LAUFI='*', XBATCH='X'}. Stored in Zagentexecution/sap_data_extraction/extracted_data/payment_process_full/TFIBLMPAYBLOCK_full.json

'Entering an asterisk in the field under the Identifier column and selecting the check box under the BRM column tells the system to send all payment runs to SAP Bank Communication Management.'

https://www.se80.co.uk/saptables/t/tfib/tfiblmpayblockid.htm — confirms TFIBLMPAYBLOCKID is 'Run Identification for Cross-Payment Run Payment Medium'. Tcode OBPM5 = 'Cross-Payment Run Payment Medium / Reservation for Cross-Payment Run Payment Media'.
